title: Horizontal Scalability Limit
description: The in-memory `sessions = new Map()` restricts the application to a single Node.js instance. Scaling horizontally will require a pub/sub mechanism (e.g., Redis) to sync WebSocket states across multiple instances.

title: DDoS / Resource Exhaustion
description: Without strict rate-limiting and connection timeouts, malicious actors could flood the server with empty WebSocket connections, eventually exhausting the Node instance's memory limit.

title: Orphaned Sessions
description: Ensure the WebSocket `on('close')` event aggressively sweeps the `sessions` Map to prevent memory leaks from dropped mobile connections.
